name: Arcifiltro
description: A silver-enhanced ceramic water filter for household drinking water treatment.

taxonomy.sub_category: Arcifiltro Updated on January 11, 2024 · Created on May 27, 2016 Arcifiltro Open-source A silver-enhanced ceramic water filter for household drinking water treatment. Developed By Asopafin Tested By Universidad de Boyacá Universidad Santiago de Cali Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Open-source Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The Arcifiltro is a silver enhanced ceramic ‘pot’ water filter for household drinking water treatment. This product is manufactured in Colombia with the help of OXFAM GB and Crediservir Foundation. It is an open source technology promoted by Potters for Peace. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $40.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Local partners: CruzRojaColombiana, Fundación Bavaria, Oxfam GB. Regions South America Countries Colombia Manufacturing/Building Method Locally sourced clay and sawdust are processed then mixed with water, pressed into the filter shape, fired to a ceramic state, flow rate tested to ensure production consistency and coated with silver nanoparticles. Intellectural Property Type Open-source User Provision Model Non-governmental organizations and individual sales Distributions to Date Status ~30,000 units as of August 2016 Type of filter Ceramic pot filter Primary material of construction Filters are manufactured with clay, sawdust and silver nanoparticles. Additives There is a potential for silver in filtered water, below secondary guideline values of 0.1 mg/L. Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 2 L/hr when full Bacteria reduction >2 Log Virus reduction Unknown Protozoa reduction Unknown Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Unknown Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) 5 NTU Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) 98-99% turbidity removal Maintenance schedule Scrub filter when flow rate slows. Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) 18,000 – 22,000 L Safe water storage integration Not available Design Specifications The product filters water through micropores of size 0.2 microns not allowing bacteria to pass. The filter is then bathed in colloidal silver that prevents the reproduction of microorganisms trapped in the filter. Specifications Diameter: 32 cm, Height: 25 cm height, Dirty water volume: 10 L; Flow rate: 2 L/hr when full. The product comes in 2 different external presentations in plastic or clay. Product Schematics Technical Support Instructions for use: To rejuvenate flow rate when filters clog, users scrub ceramic filtering element with a brush. Replacement Components Available in country: tap for dispensing water, safe storage container, ceramic filtering element. Lifecycle ~2 years Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters >2 log bacteria reduction, 2 L/hr flow rate. Vetted Performance Status Comparative 14 month laboratory evaluation: Long-term evaluation of the performance of four point-of-use water filters. Safety Clean the filter regularly for longer life Complementary Technical Systems Pre-filter or settle very turbid water (>5 NTU). Academic Research and References Comparative 14 month laboratory evaluation: Long-term evaluation of the performance of four point-of-use water filters . “Long-term evaluation of the performance of four point-of-use water filters,” CABI Databases.
